title: Date based status change automation not working retroactively
excerpt: I have an excel list of action items I am migrating to Monday. Its a list of tasks with a starting date and a due date. I am creating an automation that changes the status to expired (color coded) or going to expire base…

posts.author: Assaf1 (Assaf)
posts.cookedHtml: I have an excel list of action items I am migrating to Monday. Its a list of tasks with a starting date and a due date. I am creating an automation that changes the status to expired (color coded) or going to expire based on the due date (status needs to change 2 weeks before expiration date or on the due date for example). In this list I have items that their due date had already passed and my assumption was that for these items Monday will automatically change the status based on the automation once I turn it on even if it is retroactively (long list and I dont want to start manually changing all statuses of already expired items) but it doesnt… Is there a way to implement this with an automation?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about just creating a filter for items with a due date of before today, then selecting all those items and changing the status of one (since you have multiple items selected, it will change ALL of them).

Desiree - www.thecleverclovers.com

posts.author: Assaf1 (Assaf)
posts.cookedHtml: I need to automate this since the person dealing with these task is not technology oriented so I need to feed him the data.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Got it. What ‘trigger’ are you using? You might need a second one of ‘When item created’ IF Date is before today, then X’

posts.author: Assaf1 (Assaf)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,

Yes, item creation trigger might work. Do you have a tutorial on how to setup something like this?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Which product (CRM/Work Management, etc) and level (Pro/Enterprise) do you have?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml: Is it a large list ie thousands or ‘just’ hundreds?

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
What about using AI on the column?

posts.cookedHtml: That would have been nice if it worked… couldnt find instructions that worked, sometimes it changed the status and sometimes it didnt
